channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel's army says it will fire air force reservists who condemned the war
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel's military said Friday it will fire air force reservists who signed a letter condemning the war in Gaza and accusing it of only serving political interests and not bringing the hostages home.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ousted South Korean President Yoon embraces supporters after leaving presidential residence
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol left the presidential residence in Seoul on Friday for his private home, a week after the Constitutional Court removed him from office over his ill-fated imposition of martial law in December.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Australian opposition leader says his home was the target of an alleged bomb plot
item.description: Australian opposition leader Peter Dutton has confirmed that his family home was the target of an alleged bomb plot but says concerns for his personal safety did not restrict his election campaigning.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israelis mark another Passover hoping for Gaza captives to be freed
item.description: NAHAL OZ, Israel (AP) — Relatives of hostages in Gaza say they feel the absence of their loved ones acutely during Passover, which commemorates the Exodus of the Israelites from Egypt and their liberation from slavery.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Britain announces more support for Ukraine's fight against Russia as Kyiv's Western backers meet
item.description: Britain has announced a “surge” of military support to Ukraine, as the war-ravaged country’s Western backers gather at NATO headquarters to drum up weapons and ammunition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Burials begin as the search ends for victims in the Dominican nightclub collapse that killed 221
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — A merengue icon, a baseball star and others killed when a cement roof collapsed at a popular nightclub in the Dominican Republic were buried Thursday, as authorities called off the search for bodies with the death toll at 221.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A US Navy captain ordered a military funeral for a kamikaze pilot during WWII. Here's why
item.description: Off the coast of Japan 80 years ago, a U.S. naval captain ordered his battleship to hold a burial at sea for a kamikaze pilot who died slamming his fighter plane into the American vessel.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Mcavoy And Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Spain’s prime minister visits China to strengthen ties with Trump's top tariff target
item.description: Spain’s Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is visiting China, the top target of U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Suman Naishadham, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ksenia Karelina, a dual US-Russian national released by Moscow in prisoner swap, arrives in US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Russian-American woman freed by Moscow arrived back in the United States late Thursday as part of a prisoner swap completed as the two countries aim to repair ties. A plane carrying Ksenia Karelina landed around 11 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel releases 10 Palestinians detained from Gaza. They say they suffered abuse
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Ten Palestinians detained from Gaza by Israeli troops were freed and returned to the territory on Thursday, saying they had suffered constant abuse while imprisoned.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine 'coalition of the willing' presses ahead with force planning amid doubts about US support
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Britain's defense minister on Thursday urged his counterparts from around 30 countries to press ahead with plans to deploy troops to Ukraine to police any future peace agreement with Russia, as questions remained over whether the United States would provide backup.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and SAM McNEIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Report: Drug lord linked to DEA agent’s death freed
item.description: One of the three leaders of the Guadalajara cartel linked to the torture-slaying of Drug Enforcement Administration Agent Enrique “Kiki” Camarena in 1985 has been released in Mexico, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs lead to soaring tensions, risks for US, China
item.description: Economic tensions between the U.S. and China are soaring after President Trump raised tariffs on Chinese products to 125 percent on Wednesday and China retaliated by raising tariffs on U.S. exports to 84 percent.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, The Hill

item.title: Democrat says Trump-Xi in 'testosterone battle' with tariff war
item.description: Sen. Jeff Merkley (D-Ore.) on Thursday likened the U.S. tariff war with China to a “testosterone battle” between President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: US ambassador to Ukraine stepping down
item.description: U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine Bridget Brink is stepping down from the role she held for nearly three years, according to the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: At least 18 dead, 120 injured in roof collapse at Dominican Republic nightclub
item.description: A roof collapse at Jet Set nightclub in the Dominican Republic killed 18 and injured 120. Rescue teams are searching for survivors, while the cause remains under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 Chinese men captured in Ukraine, Zelenskyy claims
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called for action in the U.S. and Europe after claiming to capture two Chinese nationals fighting in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea says 10 North Korean soldiers crossed military demarcation line, warning shots fired
item.description: South Korea's military said troops aired warning broadcasts and fired warning shots after approximately 10 North Korean soldiers crossed the military demarcation line in the demilitarized zone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prince Harry appeals the loss of his UK security detail
item.description: Harry lost his government-funded protection in February 2020 after he stepped down from his role and moved to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills a journalist in a media tent outside a Gaza hospital
item.description: Israel struck tents outside two major hospitals in the Gaza Strip overnight, killing at least two people, including a local reporter, and wounding nine, including six journalists, Palestinian medics said. Separate strikes killed at least 15 others across the Gaza Strip, according to hospitals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel controls 50% of Gaza after razing land to expand its buffer zone
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel has dramatically expanded its footprint in the Gaza Strip since relaunching its war against Hamas last month. It now controls more than 50% of the territory and is squeezing Palestinians into shrinking wedges of land.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels killed at least six people, the group said Sunday, while a bombing video posted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas terror outlet quietly cuts Gaza death count, reveals most killed were combat-age men
item.description: New analysis shows thousands of names were removed from the Gaza casualty list as Hamas terror organization admits most deaths aged between 13 to 55 were males.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope appears in St. Peter's Square for first time in weeks
item.description: Pope Francis greeted crowds in St. Peter's Square on Sunday, his first public appearance since being discharged from the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN global comms arm under fire for anti-Israel bias as critics call for reforms
item.description: The U.N.'s Department of Global Communications is under fire for a one-sided view of the Israel-Palestinian conflict, with critics saying it often sides with Palestinians at the expense of other conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New ecosystem found after Chicago-sized chunk of Antarctica breaks off
item.description: They found animal and marine life as deep as nearly 4,300 feet. The species in the newly discovered ecosystem include crustaceans, sponges, corals and octopus.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Sick sea lions, other mammals stranded on beaches in Baja, Southern California
item.description: Officials are reporting an unusually high number of beached animals due "domoic acid or phytotoxin" in the water.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation
